data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Netflix uses microservices to break its application into smaller, independent services.
data.technical_spec: Services are deployed independently using containers. Each service manages a specific business capability.

data.title: Cloud-based Infrastructure
data.description: The platform relies heavily on cloud computing services for scalability and resilience.
data.technical_spec: Hosted primarily on AWS, utilizing EC2, S3, and RDS.

data.title: Global CDN Deployment
data.description: Content delivery network to ensure fast streaming worldwide.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes Open Connect, a custom Netflix CDN, distributed globally.

data.title: Multi-region Redundancy
data.description: Operates across multiple cloud regions for high availability and disaster recovery.
data.technical_spec: Data replication and failover strategies are implemented.

data.title: Event-Driven Architecture
data.description: Loose coupling within applications allowing for real-time data processing.
data.technical_spec: Employs Apache Kafka for streaming and event processing.
